perturbation.original_text: A Confidential Information@ shall mean any and all information that a Party may reasonably expect to remain confidential and not shared with the general public at any time or that information that is protected by law.
perturbation.changed_text: A Confidential Information@ shall mean information shared between the parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Confidential Information' emphasizes its protected and non-public nature. The modified definition removes these safeguards, now defining confidential information as simply any information shared between the parties. This change contradicts sections that outline responsibilities for protecting Confidential Information as no level of protection is specified. Furthermore, it introduces ambiguity regarding which shared information is subject to the confidentiality obligations specified in the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: It is anticipated that the Business Affiliate will provide services both for clients of the Business Affiliate and UDC that involve the use and disclosure of Confidential Information (the "Services"). Except as otherwise specified herein, the Parties may make any and all uses of Confidential Information necessary to perform the Services. Additionally, the Parties may disclose Confidential Information for the purposes authorized by this Agreement only (a) to its employees, subcontractors and agents, in accordance with this Agreement, or (b) as directed by the other Party. The Parties expressly agree that any and all uses or disclosures of the Confidential Information by a Party will be done in accordance with the terms of this Agreement and the provisions of all applicable federal and state laws and regulations.
perturbation.justification: The modified text redefines 'Confidential Information' as simply 'information shared between the parties,' removing the original definition's emphasis on protection and non-public nature. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text,' which outlines specific obligations and restrictions regarding the use and disclosure of Confidential Information. Since the modified definition encompasses all shared information without specifying confidentiality requirements, it creates a strong in-text contradiction, as the duties imposed by the 'Contradicted Text' become unclear and potentially unenforceable. The change falls under 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction', as the removal of essential language regarding the nature of Confidential Information creates conflict.

perturbation.original_text: Return to a Party or destroy, as requested by a Party, within fifteen (15) days of the termination of this Agreement, any Confidential Information provided to such Party and in its possession and retain no copies or back-up tapes
perturbation.changed_text: Return to a Party or destroy, as requested by a Party, any Confidential Information provided to such Party and in its possession and retain no copies or back-up tapes
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the phrase 'within fifteen (15) days of the termination of this Agreement' there is no deadline provided for how long until the party receiving should return/destroy the confidential information after the termination of this agreement. This contradicts Section 7(a) Survival that states that respective rights and obligations of Business Affiliate and UDC under the provisions of Sections 3(g) [Responsibilities of the Parties] and 3(h) [Responsibilities of the Parties] and 6 shall survive the termination of this Agreement indefinitely.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Survival. The respective rights and obligations of Business Affiliate and UDC under the provisions of Sections 3(g) [Responsibilities of the Parties] and 3(h) [Responsibilities of the Parties] and 6 shall survive the termination of this Agreement indefinitely.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a 15-day timeframe for returning or destroying confidential information after termination. The changed text removes this timeframe. The contradicted text states that the obligations under Section 6 survive termination indefinitely. Without a specific timeframe, as was present in the original text, this creates a contradiction because the obligation to return/destroy the information now lacks a deadline, potentially conflicting with the intent of the Survival clause, which suggests that the obligation exists but does not define a time to execute it after termination, leading to unenforceability. Therefore, the modified text creates an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Failure to cure in the manner set forth in this paragraph is grounds for the immediate termination of the Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Failure to attempt to cure in the manner set forth in this paragraph is grounds for the immediate termination of the Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text provides grounds for immediate termination if the breach is not actually 'cured'. The changed text states that attempting to cure but ultimately failing still causes grounds for immediate termination, as the word 'attempt' has been introduced. This would imply that UDC can terminate the agreement even if the Business Affiliate is trying to remedy the breach, but fails. This contradicts the idea of providing an opportunity to 'cure' the breach, since failure to fix still causes immediate termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Termination. UDC may immediately terminate this Agreement without notice and any related agreement if it determines that the Business Affiliate has breached a material provision of this Agreement or if the activities contemplated under this Agreement will result in a violation of any federal or state law or if such Services would, in the opinion of UDC and its counsel. Alternatively, UDC may choose to: (i) provide the Business Affiliate with ten (10) days written notice of the existence of an alleged material breach; and (ii) afford the Business Affiliate an opportunity to cure said alleged material breach upon mutually agreeable terms. Failure to attempt to cure in the manner set forth in this paragraph is grounds for the immediate termination of the Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that 'Failure to attempt to cure' is grounds for immediate termination, while the contradicted text allows for an 'opportunity to cure'. By stating that a mere attempt to cure, even if unsuccessful, can result in termination, the changed text contradicts the purpose and spirit of providing an opportunity to cure, because the company will still terminate even if a good-faith attempt was made to remedy the breach. This creates a direct conflict in the text.
